This is a <strong data-start="1256" data-end="1284">high-impact, hunter role</strong> that sits at the heart of our go-to-market strategy. Whilst the UKI Sales team comprises of 4 quota-carrying reps, you'll be supported by amazing colleagues across the Sales Engineering, Partner, Customer Success, BDR, Marketing &amp; People Team(s). With over 50 employees in the UKI region &amp; many local, International &amp; Global Leaders based in the UK - you'll also have great access to senior stakeholders and wider resources.&nbsp;</p> <p class="" data-start="1339" data-end="1520">Your target?&nbsp;<strong data-start="1471" data-end="1520">New logos. Big wins. Category-defining deals.</strong></p> <h4 class="" data-start="1527" data-end="1550"><strong data-start="1532" data-end="1550">What You'll Do:</strong></h4> <ul data-start="1551" data-end="2265"> <li solution mapping, negotiation, and close</p> </li> <li class="" data-start="1657" data-end="1758"> <p class="" data-start="1659" data-end="1758">Develop and execute a <strong data-start="1681" class="" data-start="1877" data-end="1980"> <p class="" data-start="1879" data-end="1980">Collaborate with internal teams to tailor solutions that address customer pain points and drive value</p> </li> <li class="" data-start="1981" data-end="2066"> <p class="" data-start="1983" data-end="2066">Leverage the Armis platform to <strong data-start="2014" data-end="2041">tell compelling title: Enterprise Account Executive - Finland
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p><strong>The role:</strong></p> <p>As an Enterprise Account Executive at Armis, you will play a crucial role in driving our growth across Australia by identifying and securing new enterprise-level business opportunities. You will be responsible for cultivating relationships with key decision-makers, understanding their cybersecurity needs, and presenting our solutions as the ideal choice to address those needs. This position offers a unique opportunity to work with cutting-edge technology, fuelled by AI and be a part of a dynamic, winning and rapidly growing local team in a company that is leading in this space.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Identify and target enterprise-level accounts to generate new business</li> <li>Leverage industry knowledge and market trends to develop a strategic approach for lead generation. Plan it out - go execute.&nbsp;</li> <li>Cross-sell and Upsell of Armis’ extensive product portfolio to existing customers&nbsp;</li> <li>Work closely with partner channels and alliances to develop new opportunities</li> <li>Build and maintain strong relationships with C-level executives, CISO’s, CIO’s, IT leaders, and other key decision-makers in your buying ecosystem.</li> <li>Conduct in-depth discussions to understand client needs, challenges, and objectives related to cybersecurity.</li> <li>Take an ‘always qualifying’ approach to maximize prospect connection.</li> <li>Effectively communicate the value proposition of our solutions to potential clients.</li> <li>Conduct product demonstrations and presentations tailored to the specific requirements of each organization.</li> <li>Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from initial contact through negotiation and closing.</li> <li>Collaborate with internal teams to ensure seamless implementation and onboarding for new clients. From Legal to Operations, Finance to Customer Success.&nbsp;</li> <li>Provide accurate and timely sales forecasts, pipeline reports, and other relevant information.</li> <li>Utilize CRM (SFDC) and other tools to track and manage all aspects of the sales process. Hygiene and accuracy is super important to us, it should be to you too.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>What we expect:</strong></p> <ul> <li>An overachiever who wants to take their sales career to the next level.</li> <li>Proven track record of significant success in enterprise-level Technology sales.</li> <li>A strong understanding of enterprise security challenges and solutions.</li> <li>Excellent communication and presentation skills.</li> <li>Ability to build and maintain relationships at the C-level.</li> <li>Self-motivated with a results-oriented &amp; process-oriented mindset.</li> <li>Ability to travel to prospects/customers/events&nbsp;</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. You want to do interesting work in an important field while also having time to live your life, which is why we place so much value in your life-work balance. Armis sets you up for success with comprehensive health benefits, discretionary time off, paid holidays including monthly <em>me </em>days, and a highly inclusive and diverse workplace. Put your unique experiences and perspective to work in an environment where they will enable you to thrive, grow, and live your life with integrity.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Armis is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We never discriminate based on race, ethnicity, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any other legally protected (or not) status. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to submit satisfactory proof of identity and legal authorization.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.armis.com/privacy-policy/" target="_blank"><span class="">Please click here to review our privacy practices.</span></a></p></div>

title: Enterprise Account Executive (Mid-Atlantic) (Application Security)
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><div class="contentGroup richTextBlock ng-scope"> <div class="userDesignedContent addPadding allowSelect allowSelectChildren ng-binding ng-scope"> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">As an Enterprise Account Executive, you will be tasked with acquiring new customers (5,000 users and above) and managing existing accounts within a specific geographic territory. You will present&nbsp; Armis’s overall business value and product capabilities to potential clients, match our strengths to our client's needs, and help our partners become trusted advisors for identity asset management. The successful candidate has established executive-level contacts and is flexible and adaptable to changing situations. You must be results-driven, customer-focused, technologically savvy, and innovative at building internal relationships and external partnerships.&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Identify, develop and execute an account strategy to close new business opportunities and expand revenue with customers across the assigned region; independently and cooperatively.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Scope, negotiate and bring to closure agreements to exceed booking and revenue quota targets.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Target and gain access to decision-makers in key prospect accounts in the assigned territory.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Establish access and maintain existing relationships with key decision-makers (typically at the CIO and CISO level) in industry, partners, and enterprise customers to drive all pertinent issues related to sales strategy and goal attainment.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Collaborate with operative peers across functions (including the Field Sales, Channel, Marketing, Sales Operations, Sales Engineering, Services, Customer Support, and Product Development) to create visibility with target accounts and drive engagement of target prospects at both the individual contributor and executive level.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Work cooperatively with partners to leverage their established account presence and relationships.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Capture, maintain, and disseminate accurate and relevant prospect information using Salesforce.com</span></li> <li>Builds and maintains a network of sources from which to identify new sales leads.</li> <li>Communicates with enterprise customers and leads to identify and understand their product or service needs; identifies and suggests products and services to meet those needs.</li> <li>Demonstrates the functions and utility of products or services to customers based on their needs.</li> <li>Ensures customer satisfaction through ongoing communication and relationship management: resolves any issues that may arise post-sale.</li> <li>Maintains communication with existing and previous customers, alerting them of new products, services, and enhancements that may be of interest.</li> </ul> </div> </div> <div class="contentGroup richTextBlock ng-scope"> <h3 class="h3-legacy ng-scope">Qualifications:</h3> <div class="userDesignedContent addPadding allowSelect allowSelectChildren ng-binding ng-scope"> <ul> <li>A minimum of 5 years of experience selling software-based solutions to senior management and executive level required</li> <li>Background in selling security software -based solutions at the Enterprise level (5,000 users and above) is required</li> <li>Proven record of achieving or exceeding assigned quota</li> <li>Referenceable customer and partner contacts within your specified geographic territory&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>The salary range guidance for this position is: $127,000-$153,000<br><br>The salary range listed does not include other forms of compensation or benefits (e.g. i.e. bonuses, commissions, stocks, health insurance benefits, etc.) offered to candidates. Visit our careers site for more&nbsp;<a href="https://www.google.com/url?q=https://www.armis.com/careers/&amp;sa=D&amp;source=calendar&amp;ust=1673891642349457&amp;usg=AOvVaw3_Qt1gwBgkDj-XzCMkPhUZ" target="_blank">information</a>&nbsp;on benefits at Armis.</p> </div> </div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. title: Head of Product Marketing
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><h3>&nbsp;</h3> <p>Armis is seeking a visionary&nbsp;<strong>Head of Product Marketing</strong>&nbsp;to lead the global strategy for our industry-leading Asset Intelligence and Security platform. In this role, you will architect our market narrative, ensuring Armis’s ability to see, protect, and manage every asset—from IT and cloud to IoT and OT—is clearly communicated to the world.</p> <p>You will oversee global growth plans for the Armis portfolio, defining our positioning in the&nbsp;<strong>Cyber Exposure Management</strong>&nbsp;category and developing a differentiated narrative around our AI-powered innovation. As a strategic partner to the executive leadership team, you will identify untapped market opportunities and leverage a deep understanding of the CISO’s pain points. Furthermore, you will lead our&nbsp;<strong>Analyst Relations</strong>&nbsp;strategy, ensuring Armis maintains a dominant "Leader" position across all relevant Gartner Magic Quadrants, Forrester Waves, and IDC MarketScapes.</p> <h3>&nbsp;</h3> <h3>What you will do:</h3> <ul> <li> <p><strong>Strategic Growth:</strong>&nbsp;Identify expansion opportunities within the Armis portfolio, specifically targeting the convergence of IT, OT, IoT, and Medical IoT (IoMT).</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Differentiated Messaging:</strong>&nbsp;Develop high-impact messaging that articulates the Armis value proposition across the entire asset attack surface, ensuring resonance with both technical buyers and C-suite executives.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Analyst Relations Leadership:</strong>&nbsp;Own the strategic relationship with industry analysts (Gartner, Forrester, IDC, etc.). Drive the briefing calendar, manage evaluation cycles, and ensure Armis’s innovation roadmap is accurately reflected in influential market reports.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Market Intelligence:</strong>&nbsp;Oversee deep-dive research into market requirements, macroeconomic trends, and the competitive landscape to keep Armis two steps ahead of legacy security vendors.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Cross-Functional Synergy:</strong>&nbsp;Partner with Product Management, Business Unit leaders, and Sales to design the "Go-to-Market" strategies and resource alignment necessary to exceed corporate ARR objectives.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Thought Leadership &amp; Brand:</strong>&nbsp;Collaborate with brand and editorial teams to elevate Armis’s storytelling, positioning the company as the definitive authority in Asset Intelligence and Cyber Exposure Management.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Content &amp; Campaign Architecture:</strong>&nbsp;Design the global content strategy for product launches, demand generation, and website presence, ensuring a consistent and compelling voice.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Sales &amp; Partner Enablement:</strong>&nbsp;Lead the creation of world-class enablement materials, equipping our global sales force and channel partners to win in complex, competitive environments.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Team Leadership:</strong>&nbsp;Provide world-class mentorship to the Product Marketing and AR teams, managing the hiring, development, and retention of top-tier marketing talent.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Executive Influence:</strong>&nbsp;Reporting to the CMO, act as a key member of the Marketing Executive team, bringing a product-centric lens to quarterly reviews and long-term strategic planning.</p> </li> </ul> <h3>&nbsp;</h3> <h3>What we expect:</h3> <ul> <li> <p><strong>Experience:</strong> 15+ years of relevant marketing experience, with a heavy focus on B2B cybersecurity or high-scale SaaS.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Education:</strong>&nbsp;BSc/MSc or MBA in Marketing, Communications, or a related technical field.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Cybersecurity:</strong>&nbsp;Deep expertise in the cybersecurity landscape, specifically in asset management, vulnerability management, or OT/IoT security.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Analyst Relations:</strong>&nbsp;Proven track record of successfully managing or collaborating with Analyst Relations and influencing "Leader" placements in major industry reports.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Growth Mindset:</strong>&nbsp;Demonstrated success in building and scaling marketing functions within hyper-growth, "Unicorn" environments.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Master Storyteller:</strong>&nbsp;The ability to distill complex, technical AI and networking concepts into memorable, "human-centric" narratives.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Systemic Thinker:</strong>&nbsp;A relationship builder who can connect the dots between Product, Sales, and Customer Success to create a unified market front.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Global Leader:</strong>&nbsp;Experience managing large, distributed teams across multiple time zones with a focus on high-velocity execution and detail.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>C-Level Presence:</strong>&nbsp;Exceptional communication skills with the confidence to influence and advise C-level executives both internally and externally.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We know there is a lot to consider when applying for a new job, and quite often job descriptions provide a lot of detail for candidates… but here at Armis, we strongly encourage you to try to avoid the ​​confidence gap​.​​ We don’t expect you to meet each of the listed requirements perfectly to be considered for any of our roles.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Salary range guidance for this position is: </strong><strong>$220,000-280,000</strong><strong> per year.</strong></p> <p><strong>The salary range listed does not include other forms of compensation or benefits (e.g. i.e. bonuses, commissions, stocks, health insurance benefits, etc.) offered to candidates. Visit our careers site for more </strong><a href="https://www.armis.com/careers/"><strong>information</strong></a><strong> on benefits at Armis.&nbsp;</strong></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. You want to do interesting work in an important field while also having time to live your life, which is why we place so much value in your life-work balance. Armis sets you up for success with comprehensive health benefits, discretionary time off, paid holidays including monthly <em>me </em>days, and a highly inclusive and diverse workplace. Put your unique experiences and perspective to work in an environment where they will enable you to thrive, grow, and live your life with integrity.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Armis is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We never discriminate based on race, ethnicity, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any other legally protected (or not) status. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to submit satisfactory proof of identity and legal authorization.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.armis.com/privacy-policy/" target="_blank"><span class="">Please click here to review our privacy practices.</span></a></p></div>

introductions, Attend joint marketing events and BDR callout days</li> </ul> <li>Partner Sales, including but not limited to:</li> <ul> <li>Ensure AE alignment, Deal Registration and Opportunity title: Sales Strategist - Application Security
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. This role focuses on one of our core products and is crucial for driving technical sales and pre-sales activities. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in technical sales or as a Sales Engineer, with a deep understanding and passion for vulnerability solutions and remediation. You’ll wear many hats in this role, quickly growing into a commercially-focussed SME for the offering.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you will do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Act as a technical overlay specialist for Application Security in the sales process. Collaborate closely with the sales team to understand customer requirements and propose solutions that effectively demonstrate the value.</li> <li>Lead and support pre-sales activities including technical presentations, demonstrations, and solution scoping. Work closely with prospects to understand their technical challenges and requirements.</li> <li>Become a subject matter expert on Application Security and related vulnerability and remediation technologies. Articulate the technical benefits and competitive advantages of Application Security to both technical and non-technical audiences.</li> <li>Engage with prospects and customers to build strong relationships and understand their pain points. Provide expert guidance on how Application Security can address their specific vulnerability management needs.</li> <li>Work closely with product management and engineering teams to provide feedback from the field and influence the product roadmap. Act as a liaison between customers and internal teams to ensure customer success.</li> <li>Stay current with industry trends, competitive landscape, and emerging technologies in vulnerability management and remediation.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What we expect...</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years in a Sales Engineer, Solutions Architect, SME overlay, or similar technical sales role within the cybersecurity industry.</li> <li>A clear track record of success against targets and quota at similar or adjacent scaling Cyber security companies.&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong understanding of vulnerability management solutions is essential. Experience with Application Security or similar competing products (inferior of course), is highly preferred.</li> <li>Excellent presentation and communication skills, both verbal and written. Ability to effectively convey technical concepts to diverse audiences.</li> <li>Proven ability to understand customer needs and build relationships based on trust and credibility.</li> <li>Genuine enthusiasm for cybersecurity, vulnerability management, and the impact of proactive remediation strategies.</li> </ul> <p>A self-motivated, independent thinker who understands the Enterprise customer segment. title: Senior Solutions Engineer – Fed IC/DoD (Active DoD TS/SCI Clearance - With CI or Lifestyle Poly required))
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p><strong>Location: </strong>United States (Remote with Travel)</p> <p><strong>Clearance Requirement: </strong>Active DoD TS/SCI Clearance - With CI or Lifestyle Poly</p> <p><strong>The Opportunity</strong><strong><br></strong>Armis is seeking a Senior Sales Engineer (SE) – Intelligence Community (IC)/DoD with an active Top Secret clearance and current CI or Lifestyle Polygraph. This customer-facing role requires 6-8+ years of IC-focused experience in networking and cybersecurity sales engineering experience. The position requires hands-on technical competency to support: installation, configuration, troubleshooting, and documentation of the pre-sales cycle. Candidates must be DC metro-based and comfortable with frequent travel.</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do</strong></p> <p>Work closely with Account Executives, prospects, and customers to:</p> <ul> <li>Deliver virtual and in-person technical demonstrations of Armis solutions</li> <li>Deploy, configure, and manage proof-of-value (POV) installations that showcase Armis capabilities that align to customer pain points and use cases</li> <li>Troubleshoot complex network environments, integrating APIs and third-party products, and ensure successful technical outcomes to the opportunity</li> <li>Lead technical training sessions on Armis solutions&nbsp;</li> <li>Design and deliver organized technical project plans that manage customer and Armis deliverables, requirements, and expectations for PoV engagements</li> <li>Support marketing initiatives, conferences, and speaking engagements as a technical subject matter expert</li> <li>Address prospect and customer needs with clear and effective verbal and written communication, including formal technical responses to queries and RFPs</li> <li>Travel frequently across the DC metro area and to federal sites as required (up to 40%)</li> </ul> <p><strong>What We Expect</strong></p> <p><strong>Education &amp; Clearance:</strong></p> <ul> <li>BS in Computer Science, Engineering, Cybersecurity, or related discipline (or equivalent experience/certifications)</li> <li>Active TS/SCI Clearance Required (with ability to obtain Lifestyle polygraph)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Technical Skills:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proven experience selling and engineering cybersecurity solutions (SaaS and on-premises) into DoD and IC environments</li> <li>Strong understanding of networking fundamentals: routing, switching, VLANs, subnets, ports and protocols, and port configuration</li> <li>Hands-on expertise with RHEL and Linux administration, containers, bare-metal builds, and system integration</li> <li>Proficiency with REST APIs, scripting, and automation (Python preferred; Bash or PowerShell acceptable)</li> <li>Familiarity with ATO/IATT&nbsp;</li> <li>Working knowledge of cybersecurity frameworks such as: NIST 800-82, RMF, DISA STIGs, etc.</li> <li>OT/ICS experience considered a strong plus</li> <li>Familiarity with Application Security and CI/CD pipelines</li> <li>Familiarity with Security Operations Center activities to include Threat Hunting and adversary tactics techniques and procedures</li> <li>Vulnerability management expertise including ACAS, Tenable/Nessus, CVEs, patch management, and CISA KEV catalog</li> </ul> <p>The salary range guidance for this position is: $160,000-$210,000</p> <p>The salary range listed does not include other forms of compensation or benefits (e.g. i.e. bonuses, commissions, stocks, health insurance benefits, etc.) offered to candidates. Visit our careers site for more <a href="https://www.armis.com/careers/">information</a> on benefits at Armis.</p> <p>The choices you make in your career journey matter. title: Senior Technical Support Engineer
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p><strong>Location:</strong> This is a hybrid position based out of Bangalore, India. 2+ days a week in office.</p> <p>Armis is making a major dent in the cybersecurity market. Joining the Armis family means working with dedicated individuals on a mission to enable large organizations to benefit from the latest connected devices without fear of compromise from a cyber attack. Armis is the leading agentless device security platform purpose-built to protect the world of unmanaged and IoT devices - providing passive, real-time, and continuous asset inventory, risk management, and detection &amp; response to prevent cyber attacks from disrupting and compromising a business.</p> <p>The Technical Support Team is at the heart of impacting and ensuring clients use the Armis products effectively. Supporting our customers to solve their challenging technical security problems is critical to achieving our mission. The technical support team does this by working with our customers to offer professional and proactive deescalation for their complex technical issues. The team collaborates and works hand in hand with the Operations, R&amp;D teams, Solution Architects, Product, Customer Success, and other teams to ensure seamless onboarding, deployment, and on-going development of our customers.</p> <p><strong>What you'll do:</strong></p> <p>Our Tier 2 Technical Support Engineer will provide second level support with a focus on English-speaking, Global customers. You’ll help pave the way as the knowledge source to our growing customer base, as you will work to resolve our customers’ most pressing technical issues. You will apply your creativity and innovative ideas that best support and create a lasting experience for our customers.</p> <p>As part of your day-to-day, you’ll take ownership of customer cases and troubleshoot customers’ issues. You will support our customers in making the most of our products. You will be taking on projects either within/with other teams to create a company-wide impact.</p> <p><strong>What we expect:</strong></p> <ul> <li>At least 8+ years of experience in a similar role</li> <li>Industry experience in Cyber Security, particularly network security or application security is required</li> <li>Experience with enterprise customer accounts is required&nbsp;</li> <li>Technical networking knowledge in TCP/IP and the application layer</li> <li>Technical experience supporting Linux-based products</li> <li>Composing SQL queries and working with Databases</li> <li>Working with Docker/Kubernetes-based environments is preferred</li> <li>Experience with Scripting languages (Bash, Python or equivalent) is preferred</li> <li>BS degree in CS or equivalent is preferred</li> <li>Enjoy working with customers using excellent communication and problem-solving skills</li> <li>Bonus: experience with ServiceNow</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. You will present&nbsp; Armis’s overall business value and product capabilities to potential clients, match our strengths to our client's needs, and help our partners become trusted advisors for identity asset management. The successful candidate has established executive-level contacts and is flexible and adaptable to changing situations. You must be results-driven, customer-focused, technologically savvy, and innovative at building internal relationships and external partnerships.&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Identify, develop and execute an account strategy to close new business opportunities and expand revenue with customers across the assigned region; independently and cooperatively.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Scope, negotiate and bring to closure agreements to exceed booking and revenue quota targets.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Target and gain access to decision-makers in key prospect accounts in the assigned territory.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Establish access and maintain existing relationships with key decision-makers (typically at the CIO and CISO level) in industry, partners, and enterprise customers to drive all pertinent issues related to sales strategy and goal attainment.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Collaborate with operative peers across functions (including the Field Sales, Channel, Marketing, Sales Operations, Sales Engineering, Services, Customer Support, and Product Development) to create visibility with target accounts and drive engagement of target prospects at both the individual contributor and executive level.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Work cooperatively with partners to leverage their established account presence and relationships.</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Capture, maintain, and disseminate accurate and relevant prospect information using Salesforce.com</span></li> <li>Builds and maintains a network of sources from which to identify new sales leads.</li> <li>Communicates with enterprise customers and leads to identify and understand their product or service needs; identifies and suggests products and services to meet those needs.</li> <li>Demonstrates the functions and utility of products or services to customers based on their needs.</li> <li>Ensures customer satisfaction through ongoing communication and relationship management: resolves any issues that may arise post-sale.</li> <li>Maintains communication with existing and previous customers, alerting them of new products, services, and enhancements that may be of interest.</li> </ul> </div> </div> <div class="contentGroup richTextBlock ng-scope"> <h3 class="h3-legacy ng-scope">Qualifications:</h3> <div class="userDesignedContent addPadding allowSelect allowSelectChildren ng-binding ng-scope"> <ul> <li>A minimum of 7 years of experience selling software-based solutions to senior management and executive level required</li> <li>Background in selling security software -based solutions at the Enterprise level (5,000 users and above) is required</li> <li>Proven record of achieving or exceeding assigned quota</li> <li>Referenceable customer and partner contacts within your specified geographic territory&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>The salary range guidance for this position is: $150,000 - $170,000<br><br>The salary range listed does not include other forms of compensation or benefits (e.g. i.e. bonuses, commissions, stocks, health insurance benefits, etc.) offered to candidates. title: Streaming Infrastructure DevOps Engineer
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p><em>An operations-first engineer with deep expertise in running, scaling, automating, and monitoring streaming infrastructure like Kafka and RabbitMQ.</em></p> <p>At Armis, streaming is at the heart of our product. We operate hundreds of streaming applications that transform, aggregate, analyze, and enrich the most valuable data we collect from our clients. We process billions of events and petabytes of raw data daily — and we’re just getting started.</p> <p>Our mission is to provide a rock-solid, scalable, and secure infrastructure foundation that empowers our engineers to build and operate streaming services with confidence. This includes managing the full lifecycle of Kafka and RabbitMQ clusters, automating deployments, securing system access, and building out observability and monitoring capabilities that scale with our growth.</p> <p>We are seeking a skilled and motivated DevOps engineer with deep familiarity in the streaming ecosystem to join our elite infrastructure team. If you're excited by the challenge of operating mission-critical systems at scale and optimizing the developer experience through automation and tooling, we’d love to hear from you.</p> <p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>What you will do</strong></span></p> <ul> <li><strong>Automate Deployment and Operation</strong><strong><br></strong> Oversee deployment of Kafka and RabbitMQ clusters (including Confluent Cloud &amp; CFK). Build automation pipelines to ensure repeatability and resiliency across environments.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Monitor and Support Production Systems</strong><strong><br></strong> Own production stability of global Kafka clusters. Handle on-call rotations, incident management, troubleshooting, and scaling challenges.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Improve Infrastructure Observability</strong><strong><br></strong> Build and maintain observability systems: dashboards, alerting pipelines, metrics collection (Prometheus, Grafana, etc.).<br><br></li> <li><strong>Optimize System Performance</strong><strong><br></strong> Collaborate with peers on benchmarking and optimization initiatives. Work on tuning Kafka brokers, cluster configurations, and runtime parameters.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Provide Developer Support and Training (Infra-focused)</strong><strong><br></strong> Help developers configure topics, quotas, and consumers appropriately. Train service owners to interpret monitoring data and avoid pitfalls.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Develop and Maintain Infrastructure</strong><strong><br></strong> Contribute to building infrastructure tools and scripts (IaC, Helm charts, etc.) that make provisioning and managing clusters reliable and efficient.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Secure Infrastructure Access</strong><strong><br></strong> Configure and maintain secure access patterns across streaming infrastructure, ensuring proper authentication and role-based access controls are enforced for both developers and services.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>What we expect</strong></span></p> <ul> <li>8+ years of experience in <strong>DevOps</strong>, <strong>SRE</strong>, or <strong>Infrastructure Engineering</strong> roles.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Deep hands-on Kafka experience</strong>, including deploying, maintaining, scaling, and monitoring clusters.<br><br></li> <li>Experience with <strong>RabbitMQ</strong>.<br><br></li> <li>Extensive experience with <strong>Docker</strong>, <strong>Kubernetes</strong>, Helm, and GitOps-style deployments.<br><br></li> <li>Infrastructure as Code experience (Terraform, Pulumi, etc.).<br><br></li> <li>Strong skills in scripting and automation (Python, Bash, etc.).<br><br></li> <li>Familiarity with <strong>Confluent Cloud</strong>, <strong>Confluent for Kubernetes</strong>, and similar tools.<br><br></li> <li>Solid understanding of authentication and authorization mechanisms in distributed systems.<br><br></li> <li>Production support mindset – with proven troubleshooting and incident resolution history.<br><br></li> <li>Collaboration and communication skills – especially with dev teams depending on platform support.<br><br></li> <li>Experience with <strong>Istio Service Mesh </strong>(bonus).<br><br></li> <li>Experience with <strong>GovCloud </strong>(bonus).<br><br></li> </ul> <h3><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Bonus Qualities:</strong></span></h3> <ul> <li>Mentorship and leadership experience in infrastructure or SRE teams.<br><br></li> <li>Contributions to automation or monitoring open-source tooling.<br><br></li> <li>Active participant in SRE or DevOps communities.<br><br></li> <li>Conference speaker or internal tech trainer.<br><br></li> <li>Technical writing about infrastructure automation or reliability.<br><br></li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. You want to do interesting work in an important field while also having time to live your life, which is why we place so much value in your life-work balance. Armis sets you up for success with comprehensive health benefits, discretionary time off, paid holidays including monthly <em>me </em>days, and a highly inclusive and diverse workplace. Put your unique experiences and perspective to work in an environment where they will enable you to thrive, grow, and live your life with integrity.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Armis is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We never discriminate based on race, ethnicity, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any other legally protected (or not) status. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to submit satisfactory proof of identity and legal authorization.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.armis.com/privacy-policy/" target="_blank"><span class="">Please click here to review our privacy practices.</span></a></p></div>

title: Technical Customer Success Manager
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p>We are looking for a&nbsp;<strong>Technical Customer Success Manager (T.CSM) (German Speaking) </strong>to join our growing Customer Success Team, who are a key in helping our valued customers achieve their strategic objectives and obtain maximum value from their investment in their Armis Platform. This will include helping in aligning new use cases to the CISO goals, and identify growth opportunities by informing the customer of our latest innovations, capabilities, and products and match these to the clients’ business goals. In addition, the <strong>T.CSM</strong> will guide the customer in the successful adoption and operationalization of the Armis platform towards maximum value realization within all levels of the organization so hands-on <strong>technical delivery of the platform</strong> and configuration is required. The <strong>T.CSM</strong> serves as the business liaison between the customer and all cross functional teams within Armis, including our Executive team, Product Management, Engineering, Sales, Training and Enablement and others.</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Manage a portfolio of up to 30 accounts throughout their lifecycle with Armis.</li> <li>Build strong customer relationships by engaging early as the customer transitions from the PreSales phase to deployment. Work with customer and Armis account teams to define success plans and strategy to meet customer’s objectives.&nbsp;</li> <li>Guide the customer through the implementation phase, delivering use cases and value, leading them to an operationalized state.</li> <li>Responsible for overall health and retention of the assigned customers, maximizing customer satisfaction (NPS scoring) and driving adoption and value for growth and reference-ability.</li> <li>Reporting Risks and Wins internally to Armis account team and externally to account sponsors and executives.</li> <li>Understand customer product issues and feature requests in order to represent the appropriate priority and voice of the customer with the Armis product team.</li> <li>Establish regular cadence (Monthly, Quarterly, Digital) with each assigned customer, to provide guidance, review executive dashboards and open issues, and communicate program status. This includes delivering Executive Business Reviews to ensure alignment on goals and value realization.&nbsp;</li> <li>Leverage Gainsight to track account activity, health score, risk, and other critical metrics.</li> <li>Maintain current functional and technical knowledge of the Armis platform and future products.</li> <li>Drive up utilization of the Armis platform and recommend additional features that can be leveraged to improve their security posture</li> <li>Build trusted relationships with relevant, technical users and manage stakeholders within assigned accounts</li> <li>Serve as a customer advocate to guide other resources at Armis towards achieving customer goals</li> <li>Validate customer deployment and configuration align with business use cases.&nbsp;</li> <li>Validate that use cases are appropriately captured and delivered in a timely manner</li> <li>Maintain knowledge of 3rd Party solutions and integrations/interoperability with the Armis platform within the assigned customer environments</li> <li>Providing technical subject matter expertise where applicable aligned to the expected proficiency&nbsp;</li> <li>Collaborating with Engineering, Product, Support and Sales teams to drive account success</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What We Expect&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Fluent in English and German (spoken and written)&nbsp;</li> <li>Possess exceptional verbal, written, social, presentation, and interpersonal skills.</li> <li>5+&nbsp; years experience in a related function with direct customer facing, advocacy and engagement experience in post-sales,systems engineering,&nbsp; professional services or client management functions, ideally in Customer Success.</li> <li>3+ years experience in network engineering, architecture, support, or network design capacity</li> <li>2+ years experience working in Security/Healthcare/automotive/Retail/OT</li> </ul> <ul> <li>Strong technical and problem solving skills coupled with the ability to provide quick resolution to problems.</li> <li>Solid project management and solution delivery experience, with demonstrated ability to drive projects from definition to final delivery.</li> </ul> <p>A plus:</p> <ul> <li>Experience as a CSM or Technical Account Manager/TAM in a Cyber Security SaaS organization.</li> <li>3+ years of experience in relationship building role including service delivery or account management in a post and/or pre sales environment</li> <li>3+ years experience as full-time Deployment Engineer highly preferred or as a Professional Services Engineer, Consultant, or IT/Operations Engineering</li> <li>5+ years experience in a combination of the following; designing, deploying, configuring, supporting, troubleshooting, debugging and coordinating Unified Communications products, devices, solutions, and technologies for small, medium, large and/or enterprise businesses</li> <li>Experience implementing a security program within enterprise organization</li> <li>Familiarity with various Healthcare/IOT devices and manufacturers</li> <li>B.S in IT, IS, CS, MIS, (or equivalent degree) and/or relevant industry qualifications (CISSP, Security +,CEH) desired</li> <li>Familiarity/Experience in Cyber Security i.e Endpoint, SOAR, SIEM</li> <li>Linux Operating System basic operation and troubleshooting (i.e. Redhat Linux /&nbsp; CentOS)</li> <li>Networking (LAN/WAN, Routing/Switching protocols, Network Security)</li> <li>Ability to travel regionally as needed (up to 20-25% per quarter)</li> <li>Additional European languages</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. You want to do interesting work in an important field while also having time to live your life, which is why we place so much value in your life-work balance. Armis sets you up for success with comprehensive health benefits, discretionary time off, paid holidays including monthly <em>me </em>days, and a highly inclusive and diverse workplace. Put your unique experiences and perspective to work in an environment where they will enable you to thrive, grow, and live your life with integrity.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Armis is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We never discriminate based on race, ethnicity, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any other legally protected (or not) status. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to submit satisfactory proof of identity and legal authorization.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.armis.com/privacy-policy/" target="_blank"><span class="">Please click here to review our privacy practices.</span></a></p></div>

title: Technical Customer Success Manager, FED TEAM
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p data-start="218" data-end="312"><strong data-start="218" data-end="256">Technical Customer Success Manager&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a <strong>Technical Customer Success Manager (T.CSM) </strong>to join our growing Customer Success Team, who are a key in helping our valued customers achieve their strategic objectives and obtain maximum value from their investment in their Armis Platform. This will include helping in aligning new use cases to the CISO goals, and identify growth opportunities by informing the customer of our latest innovations, capabilities, and products and match these to the clients’ business goals. In addition, the <strong>T.CSM</strong> will guide the customer in the successful adoption and operationalization of the Armis platform towards maximum value realization within all levels of the organization so hands-on <strong>technical delivery of the platform</strong> and configuration is required. The <strong>T.CSM</strong> serves as the business liaison between the customer and all cross functional teams within Armis, including our Executive team, Product Management, Engineering, Sales, Training and Enablement and others.</p> <p data-start="704" data-end="962">We are highly collaborative and believe that success in one area can be replicated across many. Our team thrives on going above and beyond to deliver exceptional results, and we’re looking for individuals who are passionate about making customers successful.</p> <h3 data-start="964" data-end="985">Responsibilities:</h3> <ul data-start="986" data-end="1785"> <li data-start="986" data-end="1108"> <p data-start="988" data-end="1108">Lead adoption and implementation plans for platinum-level customers, ensuring value realization from the Armis platform.</p> </li> <li data-start="1109" data-end="1222"> <p data-start="1111" data-end="1222">Understand customer use cases and use cases and are delivered on schedule.</p> </li> <li data-start="1518" data-end="1623"> <p data-start="1520" data-end="1623">Stay current on third-party integrations and how they interact with Armis within customer environments.</p> </li> <li data-start="1624" data-end="1697"> <p data-start="1626" data-end="1697">Serve as a subject matter expert in your area of technical proficiency.</p> </li> <li data-start="1698" data-end="1785"> <p data-start="1700" data-end="1785">Partner with Product, Engineering, Support, and Sales teams to drive account success.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="1787" data-end="1806">Qualifications:</h3> <ul data-start="1807" data-end="2524"> <li data-start="1807" data-end="1938"> <p data-start="1809" data-end="1938">Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field, or relevant certifications (CISSP, Security+, CEH).</p> </li> <li data-start="1939" data-end="2061"> <p data-start="1941" data-end="2061">2+ years experience in a Customer Success Manager, Technical Account Manager, or related customer-facing technical role.</p> </li> <li data-start="2062" data-end="2141"> <p data-start="2064" data-end="2141">5+ years experience in network engineering, architecture, support, or design.</p> </li> <li data-start="2142" data-end="2255"> <p data-start="2144" data-end="2255">5+ years experience in service delivery or technical account management in post- and/or pre-sales environments.</p> </li> <li data-start="2256" data-end="2344"> <p data-start="2258" data-end="2344">Strong communication and presentation skills across technical and executive audiences.</p> </li> <li data-start="2345" data-end="2425"> <p data-start="2347" data-end="2425">3+ years experience in IT, security, healthcare tech, or similar environments.</p> </li> <li data-start="2426" data-end="2524"> <p data-start="2428" data-end="2524">Familiarity with network protocols across OT/ICS, biomedical, or traditional IT infrastructures.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="2526" data-end="2543">Bonus Points:</h3> <ul data-start="2544" data-end="2824"> <li data-start="2544" data-end="2614"> <p data-start="2546" data-end="2614">Knowledge of title: Tier 2 Technical Support Engineer
description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Armis, <strong>the cyber exposure management &amp; security company</strong>, protects the entire attack surface and manages an organization’s cyber risk exposure in real time.&nbsp;In a rapidly evolving, perimeter-less world, Armis ensures that organizations&nbsp;continuously <strong>see</strong>, <strong>protect</strong> and <strong>manage</strong> all critical assets - from the<strong> ground to&nbsp;the cloud</strong>. Armis secures Fortune 100, 200 and 500 companies as well as&nbsp;national governments, state and local entities to help keep critical&nbsp;infrastructure, economies and society stay safe and secure 24/7.</p> <p>Armis is a&nbsp;privately held company headquartered in California.</p></div><p><strong>Location:</strong> This is a hybrid position based out of Bangalore, India. 2+ days a week in office.</p> <p>Armis is making a major dent in the cybersecurity market. Joining the Armis family means working with dedicated individuals on a mission to enable large organizations to benefit from the latest connected devices without fear of compromise from a cyber attack. Armis is the leading agentless device security platform purpose-built to protect the world of unmanaged and IoT devices - providing passive, real-time, and continuous asset inventory, risk management, and detection &amp; response to prevent cyber attacks from disrupting and compromising a business.</p> <p>The Technical Support Team is at the heart of impacting and ensuring clients use the Armis products effectively. Supporting our customers to solve their challenging technical security problems is critical to achieving our mission. The technical support team does this by working with our customers to offer professional and proactive deescalation for their complex technical issues. The team collaborates and works hand in hand with the Operations, R&amp;D teams, Solution Architects, Product, Customer Success, and other teams to ensure seamless onboarding, deployment, and on-going development of our customers.</p> <p><strong>What you'll do:</strong></p> <p>Our Tier 2 Technical Support Engineer will provide second level support with a focus on English-speaking, Global customers. You’ll help pave the way as the knowledge source to our growing customer base, as you will work to resolve our customers’ most pressing technical issues. You will apply your creativity and innovative ideas that best support and create a lasting experience for our customers.</p> <p>As part of your day-to-day, you’ll take ownership of customer cases and troubleshoot customers’ issues. You will support our customers in making the most of our products. You will be taking on projects either within/with other teams to create a company-wide impact.</p> <p><strong>What we expect:</strong></p> <ul> <li>At least 2 years of experience in a similar role</li> <li>Industry experience in Cyber Security, particularly network security or application security is required</li> <li>Experience with enterprise customer accounts is required&nbsp;</li> <li>Technical networking knowledge in TCP/IP and the application layer</li> <li>Technical experience supporting Linux-based products</li> <li>Composing SQL queries and working with Databases</li> <li>Working with Docker/Kubernetes-based environments is preferred</li> <li>Experience with Scripting languages (Bash, Python or equivalent) is preferred</li> <li>BS degree in CS or equivalent is preferred</li> <li>Enjoy working with customers using excellent communication and problem-solving skills</li> <li>Bonus: experience with ServiceNow</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The choices you make in your career journey matter. You want to do interesting work in an important field while also having time to live your life, which is why we place so much value in your life-work balance. Armis sets you up for success with comprehensive health benefits, discretionary time off, paid holidays including monthly <em>me </em>days, and a highly inclusive and diverse workplace. Put your unique experiences and perspective to work in an environment where they will enable you to thrive, grow, and live your life with integrity.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Armis is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We never discriminate based on race, ethnicity, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any other legally protected (or not) status. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to submit satisfactory proof of identity and legal authorization.</span></p> <p><a href="https://www.armis.com/privacy-policy/" target="_blank"><span class="">Please click here to review our privacy practices.</span></a></p></div>

title:
Senior Director Finance, France & BeLux
Levallois-Perret, Île-de-France Region, France
description: SEARCH JOBS Senior Director Finance, France & BeLux Location Levallois-Perret, Île-de-France Region, France Job ID R-254034 Date posted 07/06/2026 AstraZeneca is a global, science-led, patient-focused biopharmaceutical company that focuses on the discovery, development and commercialization of prescription medicines for some of the world’s most serious diseases. But we’re more than one of the world’s leading biopharmaceutical companies. We’re also a Great Place to Work. This is a collaborative environment where people are empowered to push the boundaries of science and unleash their entrepreneurial spirit. Welcome to an inclusive culture that champions diversity and collaboration. Experience the benefits of lifelong learning, growth and development. There’s no better place to make a difference to medicine, patients and society – and there’s no better time to join our exciting journey to pioneer the future of healthcare. Role Overview:The Senior Director Finance, France & BeLux, provides financial oversight and leadership for Alexion’s French business, partnering with the VP & General Manager to drive profitable, sustainable growth. The role requires both financial expertise and broad business leadership, strong communication at local, regional, and corporate levels, and ensures implementation of corporate financial policies and procedures. This is a c.$250m cluster and plays a significant role in the global RDU ecosystem. Key Responsibilities Planning, reporting and decision support: Act as the point of contact for local teams, regional teams and the Global Head of Financial Processes regarding business outlooks within the territory. Prepare strategic plans, annual budgets and monthly forecasts in accordance with timelines and ensure accuracy. Provide management with accurate monthly financial reports on revenue and expenditure. Implement business forecasting models for revenue projections and ensure accurate quarterly forecasts. Identify and quantify key risks and opportunities for the company in each forecasting cycle across all financial areas and liaise with Global Heads of Financial Processes (e.g. tax optimisation, accounting, etc.) Responsible for the accuracy of forecasts for the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow. Act as a business partner to the General Manager, attend local management and executive team meetings to support decision-making (e.g. analysis of options, P&R files, etc.) Ensure that the local management team fully understands the financial impact of key decisions. Establish a robust process to accurately forecast changes in headcount and quantify their impact on forecasts and budgets. Financial Control Ensure compliance with legal and tax reporting requirements through financial controls and training. Compliance controls – process improvement and policy compliance Ensure that Alexion’s financial policies and procedures are documented, communicated and implemented. Provide a robust control environment ensuring quality (SOX) and communication throughout the organisation, including, where applicable, the reporting of potential internal control issues. With the support of local and shared services teams, ensure that local statutory accounts comply with local regulations and are filed within the required deadlines. Lead the development of local finance policies, procedures and best practices. Leverage shared services for all administrative and transactional support, seeking to implement processes and solutions in the countries wherever possible. Accounting Responsible for reviewing and approving monthly and quarterly financial figures (Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) in accordance with Alexion’s financial reporting guidelines. Provide a robust control environment ensuring quality (SOX) and communication across the organisation. Ensure that local statutory accounts comply with local GAAP and are filed within the required deadlines. Proactively drive process improvements that have a positive impact at national and/or regional level Overall responsibility for the financial requirements of the legal entity, including, for example, statutory financial statements and shareholder resolutions on treasury matters such as dividend distributions. Tax Responsible for approving all types of tax (transfer pricing, direct and indirect taxes), following the preparation of documentation by the Tax team. Enhancing understanding of non-deductible expenses, implementing measures to minimise their impact, and supporting the tax department in developing effective strategies to reduce tax rates. Manage local tax audits Ensure the accuracy of entities’ profit forecasts and manage inter-company transfer pricing where necessary. Experience and qualifications 10 years’ experience in Finance / Management Accounting; experience in a leading consultancy firm is a plus, as is experience in a rapidly growing company. Experience of managing a finance team, ideally with similar responsibilities in business partnerships, financial reporting and control. A professional accounting qualification Experience within international organisations Experience in the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industry is a significant asset. Good IT skills (Excel, PowerPoint) Knowledge of an ERP system, such as SAP or similar Fluency in the French language and written and spoken English is essential Date Posted 08-Jun-2026 Closing Date 21-Jun-2026 Alexion is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We are committed to fostering a culture of belonging where every single person can belong because of their uniqueness. The Company will not make decisions about employment, training, compensation, promotion, and other terms and conditions of employment based on race, color, religion, creed or lack thereof, sex, sexual orientation, age, ancestry, national origin, ethnicity, citizenship status, marital status, pregnancy, (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), parental status (including adoption or surrogacy), military status, protected veteran status, disability, medical condition, gender identity or expression, genetic information, mental illness or other characteristics protected by law. Alexion provides reasonable accommodations to meet the needs of candidates and employees. To begin an interactive dialogue with Alexion regarding an accommodation, please contact accommodations@Alexion.com. Alexion participates in E-Verify. title:
Medical Science Liaison, Rare Hematology/Nephrology, Virginia, Georgia, N. Carolina, S. Carolina
Wilmington, Delaware
description: SEARCH JOBS Medical Science Liaison, Rare Hematology/Nephrology, Virginia, Georgia, N. Carolina, S. Carolina Location Wilmington, Delaware, United States Job ID R-252062 Date posted 07/06/2026 This is what you will do: The Medical Science Liaison (MSL) is a field-based scientific expert that strategically supports the medical and scientific objectives of Alexion, AstraZeneca Rare Disease’s product/s across the product/s life cycle. The individual is a peer scientific expert who communicates accurate and applicable science to Healthcare Practitioners (HCPs) within a geographic area. The individual manages and maintains collegial and professional relationships with HCPs and Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and identifies and facilitates collaboration opportunities with Alexion, AstraZeneca Rare Disease. The MSL combines strong business acumen and clinical expertise and serves as a peer-to-peer liaison internally to Medical Directors, USMA Leadership Team, cross-functional partners and externally (HCPs & KOLs). The MSL should be able to identify problems early on, seek appropriate help and counsel to work past any barriers, and innovate in order to achieve medical goals and objectives. You will be responsible for: HCP relationship management Evaluates their territory to profile and identify the HCPs to engage and build relationships with, as well as identify, important centers of excellence Develops clear, succinct, impactful HCP engagement plans, aligned with medical strategic goals and objectives. Evaluates and refines plans as needed per the progress against the quarterly focus priorities Gains HCPs’ trust and respect by effectively providing and communicating relevant scientific/medical information on the company’s products and disease states Gathers and disseminates HCP insights as appropriate internally to help inform medical strategy Develops strong working relationships in different external business settings Scientific knowledge and training Keeps current with scientific knowledge within assigned products and therapeutic area Supports scientific exchange of product/disease state with external stakeholders (HCPs) as needed Provides relevant scientific information to other internal teams and functions as appropriate Demonstrates a functional understanding of real-world evidence and HEOR Research & Development Demonstrates a strong understanding of clinical study design and protocols Ensures HCPs and stakeholders receive focused and balanced clinical and scientific information Develops expertise in applicable research outcomes to credibly conduct effective discussion with HCPs Supports applicable externally sponsored research and clinical trials, as appropriate US Healthcare system knowledge Acquires knowledge of the current US healthcare system environment and its impact on the rare disease ecosystem Builds a basic understanding of the foundations of managed markets Acquires a basic understanding of the flow of information in managed care and government organizations You will need to have: Minimum Education MD, PharmD, or PhD in biological sciences strongly preferred Clinical experience in a relevant therapeutic area OR track record of success as an MSL Minimum Experience Industry experience is preferred but not mandatory; if no industry experience, therapeutic area experience (Hematology/Nephrology/Transplant) is preferred Specific Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Has excellent communication skills (oral and written) Energetically embraces responsibilities and demonstrates the ability to achieve goals Has a strong personal initiative Functions well as part of a cross-functional team Exhibits excellent time management Able to work independently Demonstrates leadership skills and high-level job competencies Additional Role Requirements Office is home based. Travel within region 3-4 days per week with 1-2 office days per week Person should be able to travel minimum ~70% time Required travel to medical meetings, team meetings and other group meetings (including possible weekends, as dictated by conference schedule) Candidate is required to live within the territory The duties of this role are generally conducted in a field environment. As is typical of a field-based role, employees must be able, with or without an accommodation, to travel by public transportation, automobile, train, or plane; work comfortably in a clinical setting; use a computer; engage in communications via phone, video, and electronic messaging; problem solve; engage in complex and non-linear thought, analysis, and dialogue; collaborate with others; maintain general availability during standard business hours At AstraZeneca, we are taking bold action on climate because we recognize the connection between healthy people and a healthy planet. As part of our flagship Ambition Zero Carbon program, we have committed to a fully electric vehicle (EV) fleet by the end of 2025, which means that all of our drivers will be assigned an EV. Ability to travel within territory on a regular basis which will include frequent overnight and weekend travel. Valid driver’s license and clean driving record required). All activities should be conducted per Alexion, AstraZeneca Rare Disease policies. Date Posted 08-Jun-2026 Closing Date 09-Jun-2026 Our mission is to build an inclusive environment where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and employees. In furtherance of that mission, we welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of their protected characteristics. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please complete the corresponding section in the application form. title:
Director, U.S. Marketing Strategy, IgAN
Boston, Massachusetts
description: SEARCH JOBS Director, U.S. Marketing Strategy, IgAN Location Boston, Massachusetts, United States Job ID R-252762 Date posted 19/05/2026 This is what you will do: The Director, U.S. Marketing Strategy, IgAN will be a key member of the U.S. commercial team and will play a critical role in translating brand strategy into integrated launch plans and execution for a new indication in IgA Nephropathy (IgAN). This role is accountable for driving execution of priority marketing initiatives and launch workstreams, ensuring alignment across customer segments, and enabling successful launch readiness and in-market performance. The Director will partner closely with cross-functional and global teams to ensure coordinated, high-quality execution aligned to U.S. brand priorities. You will be responsible for: Translating U.S. brand and launch strategy into integrated launch plans and execution, across customer segments Driving execution of prioritized launch workstreams aligned to brand strategy, including disease education, omnichannel programs, and field readiness Operationalizing brand positioning, value proposition, and messaging platform across marketing programs and customer touchpoints Executing omnichannel marketing initiatives across personal and non-personal channels to support disease awareness, diagnosis, patient identification, and treatment adoption Supporting execution of market shaping initiatives, including disease awareness, diagnostic pathway optimization, and stakeholder engagement to accelerate appropriate patient identification and treatment Generating and applying insights from market research, analytics, and field feedback to refine execution and improve performance Partnering with cross-functional teams (Medical, Market Access, Sales, Analytics, Patient Services, Regulatory, Legal, Compliance, and Global) to align around strategy and ensure coordinated execution Supporting the Sr. Director in developing brand positioning, value proposition and evidence translation, ensuring clear and compelling communication across stakeholders Defining, tracking, and reporting key performance metrics, identifying optimization opportunities and driving continuous improvement Leading development and execution of marketing programs and materials, ensuring high quality, compliance, and alignment with brand strategy Managing agencies, budgets, and project timelines, ensuring efficient execution and strong operational discipline Partnering with Sales and Training teams to support field readiness and effective execution at launch Identifying and resolving executional risks, ensuring timely delivery of launch milestones Driving a culture of collaboration, accountability, and execution excellence across internal and external partners You will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in life sciences, business, or related field 8+ years’ experience in Biotech/pharma, with at least 3+ years in rare disease or specialty marketing Strong understanding of rare disease or specialty market dynamics, including patient journey, HCP decision-making, and access considerations Proven ability to translate strategy into actionable, measurable marketing plans and deliver high-quality execution Demonstrated success managing complex, cross-functional initiatives in a matrixed environment Strong analytical capabilities and ability to leverage data and insights to optimize performance Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills Strong project management skills with ability to manage multiple priorities and timelines Ability to engage effectively with scientific, clinical, and commercial stakeholders Ability to travel as required to support business needs up to ~30% The duties of this role are generally conducted in an office environment. As is typical of an office-based role, employees must be able, with or without accommodation, to: use a computer; engage in communications via phone, video, and electronic messaging; engage in problem solving and non-linear thought, analysis, and dialogue; collaborate with others; maintain general availability during standard business hours. We would prefer for you to have: Advanced degree (MBA, PharmD, PhD, or MS) Experience in nephrology, rare disease, or specialty therapeutics Prior experience in launch strategy development and market shaping Experience working across both HCP and patient engagement strategies Demonstrated experience integrating data, analytics, and digital capabilities into marketing execution The annual base pay for this position ranges from $ 194,126.40 - 291,189.60 Annual USD. Base pay offered may vary depending on multiple individualized factors, including market location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. In addition, our positions offer a short-term incentive bonus opportunity; eligibility to participate in our equity-based long-term incentive program. Benefits offered include a qualified retirement program [401(k) plan]; paid vacation and holidays; paid leaves; and, health benefits including medical, prescription drug, dental, and vision coverage in accordance with the terms and conditions of the applicable plans. Additional details of participation in these benefit plans will be provided if an employee receives an offer of employment. If hired, employee will be in an “at-will position” and the Company reserves the right to modify base pay (as well as any other discretionary payment or compensation program) at any time, including for reasons related to individual performance, Company or individual department/team performance, and market factors.
